transformation_type: multicomponent amine alkylation using a boronic acid nucleophile and carbonyl-derived iminium ion
mechanism_type: iminium formation followed by boronate ate-complex generation and vinyl/aryl transfer
reactant_pattern_text: amine + aldehyde or ketone + boronic acid or boronate
product_pattern_text: allylic amine, benzylic amine, or amino alcohol derivative
common_conditions: often preforms an iminium from the amine and carbonyl partner, then adds the boronic acid under mild heating or room-temperature conditions
description_short: Boronic-acid-mediated Mannich variant that joins amines, carbonyl compounds, and organoboron nucleophiles to make allylic or benzylic amines under mild conditions.

transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted rearrangement of enol acetals to substituted tetrahydrofurans or tetrahydropyrans
mechanism_type: stepwise oxocarbenium rearrangement followed by intramolecular aluminum-mediated reduction
reactant_pattern_text: five- or six-membered enol acetal derived from 1,3-dioxolan-4-one or 1,3-dioxane-4-one
common_conditions: enol acetal generation is followed by Lewis-acid-mediated rearrangement, often using organoaluminum reagents to control stereochemistry and carbonyl transposition
description_short: Rearrangement manifold that converts cyclic enol acetals into highly substituted oxygen heterocycles and is especially useful for tetrahydropyran construction in polyketide synthesis.

transformation_type: olefination of carbonyl compounds via α-silyl carbanions and β-hydroxysilane elimination
mechanism_type: nucleophilic addition to carbonyl followed by acid- or base-triggered stereospecific elimination
product_pattern_text: alkene or enyne after elimination from β-hydroxysilane
common_conditions: requires formation of a β-hydroxysilane intermediate, with acid and base conditions often giving complementary alkene geometries
description_short: Silicon-based olefination method that turns carbonyl compounds into alkenes through β-hydroxysilane intermediates with condition-dependent E/Z control.

transformation_type: activated-DMSO oxidation of primary or secondary alcohols to carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: acid-activated carbodiimide/DMSO pathway via alkoxysulfonium ylide formation and decomposition
reactant_pattern_text: 1° or 2° alcohol + DMSO + carbodiimide + catalytic acid
common_conditions: mild weakly acidic oxidation conditions that often use DCC and catalytic acid, with variants including Albright-Goldman, Parikh-Doering, and Swern conditions
description_short: Classical activated-DMSO oxidation that converts alcohols to aldehydes or ketones under mild conditions using carbodiimides and catalytic acid.

transformation_type: acid-promoted cyclocondensation of β-arylethylamines with carbonyl compounds to tetrahydroisoquinolines
mechanism_type: Schiff-base or iminium formation followed by intramolecular electrophilic aromatic substitution and deprotonation
common_conditions: typically relies on acid-promoted iminium generation and cyclization of an electron-rich aryl or heteroaryl ring, sometimes from acetal surrogates
description_short: Foundational alkaloid-forming cyclization that converts β-arylethylamines and carbonyl partners into tetrahydroisoquinoline frameworks through iminium capture.
